summary: Slice 5 HELD — fresh-clone Python X1 found a GENUINE shipped-engine regression: multi-doc ingest_with_extractor broken
detail: Slice 5 returned 22 commits on orch-0.8.20-s5 @ 9898fd8e, local-only. Steward re-verified the gates rather than accepting them: cargo check AND clippy --workspace --all-targets both exit 0 with zero warnings; SCHEMA_VERSION 20->21; AC-041 intact (diffed for recovery_denylist and the five names across all .rs — ZERO +/- lines touch it); primary checkout unwritten; shared .venv intact (fathomdb.pth mtime Jul 9, still -> main repo) so TC-27's autonomous maturin did no damage. codex 9 terminal PASS over 4 rounds. LANDING WITHHELD. The orchestrator flagged Python X1 as a landing blocker because maturin develop cannot run from a worktree, so ~50 SourceId-swept fixtures were STATICALLY checked only. Steward resolved this by commissioning a FRESH-CLONE verification with its own venv (the 0.8.19 X1 pattern — touches neither the primary checkout nor the shared .venv) rather than escalating it as a question. RESULT: the fixture sweep itself is genuinely correct (all ~50 pass; erase_source/provenance/idspace-parity 16/16), BUT the static-only check MASKED A REAL ENGINE REGRESSION. Multi-document ingest_with_extractor is BROKEN on the branch: A/B verified (fresh build of main f22e4947 = multi-doc OK; Slice-5 = _fathomdb.ExtractorError). Steward confirmed the mechanism in source: resolve_provenance (engine :3922-3935) admits the model's echo ONLY as a selector among caller-supplied batch ids and hard-fails a multi-doc batch whose entity carries no recognised echo (:3972), while the shipped harness src/python/eval/elps_live_harness.py:212-214 backfills source_doc_id onto EDGES ONLY and _STUB_ENTITIES (:95) carries none. The ENGINE LOGIC IS CORRECT AND MUST NOT BE WEAKENED — the echo is attacker-controlled and accepting it as a value is exactly how a row acquires an unerasable NULL source_id (the R-20-E2 defect). The gap is that the EXTRACTOR PROTOCOL never required per-entity attribution, so the engine now demands an echo the contract does not promise. Fix dispatched to the orchestrator: symmetric entity backfill + prompt/schema requirement + a multi-doc RED test (absent coverage is why this got through — existing tests were single-doc) + document that multi-doc batches now require per-entity attribution. ALSO CONFIRMED LIVE AND PRE-EXISTING (not Slice 5, fails identically on main): SearchHit.source_id returns None on the search path, so provenance is NOT exposed to SDK callers, and SearchHit.id being IdSpace makes int(sh.id) raise at eval/r2_parity_eval.py:325 — this is the known-OPEN 'NO SDK exposure' erasure-axis defect, now with a live reproduction; needs its own placement. Erasure itself VERIFIED COMPLETE at rest: token present in 5 raw cells before erase_source, absent from all 44 tables after, 0 byte-level hits in the db file, WAL checkpointed, unrelated rows survive. TC-20 (eu7 guard shape): steward WITHDREW its own #[ignore]-hazard objection — that reasoning assumed eu7 still gates something, and R-20-EU7 is closed by HITL decision, so #[ignore] is simply safe; folded into the fix round.

summary: codex verified the 3 steward positions: P1 CONFIRMED, P2 PARTLY (STEWARD ERROR — ADR-0.8.11 D3 DOES exist), P3 CONFIRMED

P2 PARTLY CONFIRMED — **STEWARD ERROR, SELF-REPORTED**: I asserted 'ADR-0.8.11 D3 does not exist. The citation is wrong.' IT DOES EXIST: dev/adr/ADR-0.8.11-filter-grammar-unification.md:213-225, 'D3 — Total backend-dispatch + typed-rejection (no silent fallback, no demotion)', which explicitly forbids demoting an indexed-metadata predicate to a post-KNN json_extract and typed-rejects FilterTerm::Json on search_filtered. Cause of my error: I grepped dev/design/*0.8.11* and dev/design/adr* and NEVER SEARCHED dev/adr/, a whole ADR directory. The Slice-15 orchestrator's citation was RIGHT and my correction of it was WRONG. Lesson: I applied 'trust git not narration' to the orchestrator but not to my own negative result — a failed grep is not proof of absence, and asserting non-existence demands an exhaustive search, not a plausible one. ALSO CORRECTED BY codex: I claimed embeddings are persisted in _fathomdb_vector_rows; they are NOT — that table holds only {rowid,kind,write_cursor} (fathomdb-schema:176). Embeddings live in vector_default.embedding (engine:12534, written by commit_projection_outcomes:11774). My CONCLUSION survived my wrong premise: reshaping vec0 does NOT inherently require re-embedding — existing migrations stage the persisted blobs, drop/recreate vector_default, and REINSERT them (engine:12549/12572/12587/12631). IMPORTANT DISTINCTION codex drew: rebuild_vec0() DOES re-embed, because run_rebuild->rebuild_shadow_state truncates vector rows and replays projectors which only ENQUEUE work, and workers then call embed()/embed_batch (engine:6775/8008/13978/11129/10946) — but a PURPOSE-BUILT reshape migration need not. NET EFFECT ON THE RECOMMENDATION (unchanged, and now better grounded): do NOT scope filterable down. Because a reshape needs no re-embed, the cheap path HONOURS D3 rather than deviating from it — no ADR amendment is required, so the deviation the orchestrator said was 'owed ratification' can simply be avoided. P3 CONFIRMED — strftime('%s',<user value>) does NOT exist in shipped code (only strftime('%s','now') at :12660; a user-value datetime(?1) exists at :4671 but is not the proposed conversion). NULL t_invalid IS treated as still valid (schema:339 states NULL='still valid'; filters at :9910/:10059/:10455 read 't_invalid IS NULL OR datetime(t_invalid) > datetime(now)'), so a conversion mapping malformed input to NULL FAILS OPEN. temporal_fallback IS matched by string equality (:4253 collects by kind=='temporal_fallback'+substituted_t_valid; :4434 checks fallback_dates.contains(tv)). Hazard = fail-open coercion + brittle string equality, NOT the LLM's ISO format.

summary: TC-46 RESOLVED — HITL picks Option 1: 15e vec0 reshape is NON-DESTRUCTIVE re-insert; TC-46 dissolves, no new governed surface
detail: HITL (coreyt) 2026-07-21 chose Option 1 after the steward REFRAMED the orchestrator's A/B/C. Reframing: all three orchestrator options assumed a runtime reshape must WIPE embeddings — a premise inherited from over-applying Decision-4 (NO-DATA-MIGRATION, which the HITL scoped to the cross-version 0.8.9->0.8.20 schema step) to a RUNTIME configure_projections reconfiguration of a live DB. Steward verified embeddings persist in vector_default.embedding and the engine already re-inserts them across reshapes. HITL asked the correctness crux: will previous+future entries stay aligned/coherent/queryable under a non-destructive reshape? Steward commissioned a read-only code investigation (agent, cf2f155c): ANSWER = YES WITH 4 CONDITIONS, and the tree ALREADY SHIPS this exact op as migrate_vector_partition_pack1_to_pack2 (engine:13492-13529). (A) ALIGNMENT coherent — rowid==write_cursor is the sole join key (:7086/:10005), re-insert lists rowid explicitly. (B) COHERENCE coherent — old rows get '' sentinel (vec0 TEXT is NOT-NULL-able :7091), cleanly fail-to-match a filter, stay queryable otherwise — PROVIDED the new col is plain-metadata/partition, NEVER a vec0 aux(+) col (aux hard-errors every filtered KNN, :13460-13464). (C) QUERYABILITY coherent ONLY if bits are copied verbatim via vec_bit — re-quantizing from raw embedding decenters old rows vs mean-centered new rows => silently-wrong Hamming, no error (:13514-13527; anti-pattern :13584-13599). MATERIAL FINDING that reframed the whole call: filterable ALREADY WORKS via the Slice-15d row-owned EAV table canonical_attributes; the vec0 reshape is ONLY the pre-KNN vector-path optimization (D3), not filterable-correctness — so Option 2 (wipe+ack-param) was clearly wrong and Option 3 (descope) loses ZERO correctness. HITL picked Option 1 (do the reshape now). CONSEQUENCE: TC-46 DISSOLVES — no reshape-acknowledgement parameter, NO NEW GOVERNED SURFACE for 15e; Decision-4 stays scoped to the cross-version step. The 4 conditions are written into plan §9 and are load-bearing. summary: Finding-1 RESOLVED (HITL, union of i+ii): ship A (edges excluded) for 0.8.20, reserve D, decline B/C; Memex consult recorded + 2 asks carried to the SDK-surface slice
detail: Finding 1 = what an attribute filter does to EDGE hits in a hybrid/graph search (attributes are node-only; edges never attribute-projected). Decided after three parallel investigations + a Memex consult. HITL directed the UNION of (i) build-closeout and (ii) decision-record. RULING: ship (A) edges-excluded as 0.8.20 behavior (already shipped after 15e fix-1), documented + test-pinned; (B) DECLINED; (C) NOT BUILT; (D) endpoint-node filtering RESERVED+documented, not built; no per-query opt-out flag. EVIDENCE: (1) STEWARD-VERIFIED that  is NOT on the Py/TS search wire (napi SearchFilterInput + py search carry only source_type/kind/created_after/status; engine:5561 'a later slice adds that surface') => attribute-filtering is engine-internal only in 0.8.20, so the edge-semantics choice is not consumer-reachable this release and (A) forecloses nothing. (2) narrow-then-wide TRACE: current shipped behavior IS (A); because attributes isn't on the wire NONE of A/B/C is an API break in any direction; (A) is pure query-time with zero stored/wire commitment and widens freely; (C) is the ONLY one-way door (stored-data commitment; retroactive backfill forbidden by the no-migration posture); narrowing (B->A, C->A) is a silent result-set regression not an API break. (3) ranking TRACE: raw (B) is mechanically coherent but SEMANTICALLY SURPRISING — RRF (fuse_three_arms, RRF_K=30) puts edges on the DOMINANT text weight 3.0, so an unfiltered edge can OUTRANK a node that satisfied the filter; and (B) would REOPEN the 15e fix-1 [P2] that made edge filtering consistent; coherent variants B'(nodes-first-then-edges) / B''(edge sidecar) exist via the TextEdge branch tag if pass-through is ever wanted.
